skill.name: vueuse-functions
skill.description: Use VueUse composables to build concise, maintainable Vue 3 and Nuxt 3 features.
skill.summary: Map Vue and Nuxt requirements to practical VueUse composable patterns.
skill.author: antfu

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many severe patterns, but targeted review shows they are primarily Markdown references, inline code, URLs, and VueUse API examples rather than executable skill logic. No prompt injection or malicious intent was found. The skill is publishable with a medium warning because it can guide users toward network, browser storage, cookie, local file, media-device, and Electron IPC APIs.

medium_findings.title: Privacy-sensitive browser storage guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends VueUse helpers for LocalStorage, SessionStorage, and cookies. These APIs are legitimate, but generated code could persist sensitive user data if the assistant applies them without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files directly document browser storage and cookie composables. The risk is contextual rather than malicious because the content is API guidance, not active data collection.

medium_findings.title: Outbound network composables require endpoint review
medium_findings.description: The skill includes automatic guidance for fetch, Axios, EventSource, and WebSocket composables. Generated implementations can send application data to external services if endpoints are not reviewed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network capability is explicit and includes example outbound URLs. There is no evidence of exfiltration intent, so this is a publishable design risk.

medium_findings.title: Local file and media device APIs need user consent checks
medium_findings.description: The skill documents composables for local file access and media capture. These are browser-permission APIs, but generated features must preserve clear user consent and narrow file or device scope.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced APIs can access local files, camera, microphone, or display streams after browser prompts. The guidance is legitimate, but misuse could create privacy risk.

medium_findings.title: Electron IPC helpers can cross trust boundaries
medium_findings.description: The skill includes Electron ipcRenderer helpers. Generated Electron code should validate channels and payloads because renderer-to-main messages can reach privileged application logic.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented API sends and invokes IPC channels. This is expected for Electron integrations, but it deserves review in desktop applications.

low_findings.title: Static external command findings are Markdown false positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution appears to come from Markdown code fences, inline code, and install examples. No executable script or dynamic command runner was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation text or fenced examples. They are not runnable skill hooks, scripts, or command execution code.

low_findings.title: Keyword-based critical findings are documentation false positives
low_findings.description: Keylogger, C2, weak crypto, credential, and path traversal alerts map to API names, URL fragments, line anchors, or ordinary documentation. No evidence of malicious collection or control flow was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sampled critical signals are normal VueUse documentation content. A prompt-injection phrase search also found no suspicious override text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network request generation pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can recommend composables that create HTTP, Axios, EventSource, or WebSocket connections. Review destinations, request data, and authentication handling before accepting generated code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code examples directly instantiate network clients. This is a normal VueUse capability but a meaningful security review point.

dangerous_patterns.title: Browser persistence pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can recommend persistent browser storage and cookie helpers. Avoid storing secrets, tokens, or personal data unless the application has a clear storage policy.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented helpers read and write browser-side persisted data. The pattern is legitimate but can become unsafe with sensitive values.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local capability API pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can recommend file-system and media-device APIs. Generated features should use browser permission prompts, narrow accept lists, and visible user actions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples request file and device access through standard browser APIs. The risk depends on application context and consent handling.

content.user_title: Apply VueUse Composables in Vue and Nuxt
content.value_statement: Vue and Nuxt teams often rewrite behavior that VueUse already solves. This skill maps requirements to proven composables and usage patterns.
actual_capabilities: Selects relevant VueUse composables for Vue 3 and Nuxt 3 tasks.
Uses included reference files for usage examples and type declarations.
Covers state, browser, sensor, network, animation, watch, and utility helpers.
Distinguishes automatic, external dependency, and explicit-only composable usage.
Encourages concise implementations instead of custom reactive boilerplate.
limitations: Requires an existing Vue 3 or Nuxt 3 project.
Does not install external VueUse integrations by itself.
Does not validate runtime browser permissions or production policies.
Reference files may lag the newest upstream VueUse release.

use_cases.title: Build reactive UI behavior faster
use_cases.description: Use VueUse helpers for storage, events, element state, timers, and browser APIs without writing custom composables.

use_cases.title: Standardize Nuxt feature patterns
use_cases.description: Guide assistants toward consistent VueUse choices when adding state, network, and lifecycle behavior across Nuxt pages.

use_cases.title: Modernize custom frontend utilities
use_cases.description: Replace bespoke reactive utilities with documented VueUse composables while preserving project conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Find a composable
prompt_templates.prompt: I am building a Vue 3 feature that tracks window size. Use this skill to choose the best VueUse composable and explain why.

prompt_templates.title: Implement a small feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a Nuxt 3 dark mode toggle using VueUse. Follow the skill references and keep the implementation minimal.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor custom logic
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Vue component and replace custom event listener or storage logic with appropriate VueUse composables.

prompt_templates.title: Design a safe integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a Vue feature that uses network and browser permission APIs. Use VueUse where helpful and call out security checks.

output_examples.input: A request to add a click-outside handler for a dropdown.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends onClickOutside, identifies the target element ref, and describes cleanup behavior handled by VueUse.

output_examples.input: A request to persist a user interface preference.
output_examples.output: The assistant suggests useStorage or useLocalStorage, warns against secrets, and names the storage key and fallback value.

output_examples.input: A request to stream camera input in a Vue component.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends useUserMedia, notes browser permission requirements, and describes start, stop, and preview handling.

best_practices: Check the matching reference file before choosing a composable.
Review storage, network, file, and media APIs for privacy impact.
Keep external integrations explicit when the dependency is not installed.
anti_patterns: Do not store tokens or personal data in browser storage by default.
Do not add network endpoints without reviewing data sent to them.
Do not force VueUse when plain Vue code is clearer.

faq.question: What projects can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is intended for Vue 3 and Nuxt 3 projects that can use VueUse composables.

faq.question: Does it install VueUse?
faq.answer: No. It guides code generation and may suggest installs when an external integration is required.

faq.question: Can it choose between similar composables?
faq.answer: Yes. It maps requirements to the closest VueUse function and checks the included reference documentation.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive browser APIs?
faq.answer: Use review. Some referenced composables touch storage, cookies, files, media devices, network requests, or Electron IPC.

faq.question: Does it generate complete Vue components?
faq.answer: It can help implement components, but the host assistant still adapts code to your project structure.

faq.question: Does it replace VueUse documentation?
faq.answer: No. It packages focused guidance and references, but official documentation remains the source for updates.
